A. Put in the correct word from the box in each gap.

1) ... phoned you today, but he didn't tell me his name.

2) I'm sure you'll find it ... in the house if you keep looking,

3) I had to go to the cinema on my own because | couldn't find ... to go with me.
4).She said that ... was fine and she was very happy.

5) Can | speak to you for a moment? I want to discuss .... with you.

6) Unfortunately, | couldn't help. There was ... I could do about the problem.

1) Someone phoned you today, but he didn't tell me his name.
2) I'm sure you'll find it somewhere in the house if you keep looking.
3) I had to go to the cinema on my own because I couldn't find anyone to go with me.
4) She said that everything was fine and she was very happy.
5) Can I speak to you for a moment? I want to discuss something with you.
6) Unfortunately, I couldn't help. There was nothing I could do about the problem.

Put in the correct word from the box in each gap. 7) I ooked .... .. but I couldn't find it.
8) She married ... she met when she was a student.
9) ... was out of the office so there was .. to answer the phone.

7) I looked everywhere but I couldn't find it.
8) She married someone she met when she was a student.
9) Everyone was out of the office so there was no one to answer the phone.

B Choose the correct verb form in brackets ( ).

1) I asked a lot of people, but nobody ... (knew/didn't know) the answer.
2) I ...  (have Seenihaven't seen) anything so lovely before in my life!
3) I ...  (ate/didn't eat) anything for lunch yesterday.
4) Nothing interesting ..... (has happened/hasn't  happened) since the last time | spoke to you.
5) He loves football. Nothing else . .. (is/isn't) important to him.
6) She ... (Said/didn't say) anything about her plans for the future.

1) I asked a lot of people, but nobody knew the answer.
2) I haven't seen anything so lovely before in my life!
3) I didn't eat anything for lunch yesterday.
4) Nothing interesting has happened since the last time I spoke to you.
5) He loves football. Nothing else is important to him.
6) She didn't say anything about her plans for the future.

Change each of these sentences into a sentence with the same meaning.
Use the word in brackets () with the underlined adjective or with else.

1) Is there an interesting programme on TV tonight? (anything)
Is there ... on TV tonight?
2) You won't find better food in any other place. (anywhere)
You won't find better food ...
3) Is there a cheap place we can go for lunch? (anywhere)
Is there ...  we can go for lunch?
4) Lets sit in a different place. (somewhere)
Let's sit ...
5) I'd like a hot drink. (something)
I'd like ... to drink.

1) Is there anything interesting on TV tonight?
2) You won't find better food anywhere else.
3) Is there anywhere cheap we can go for lunch?
4) Let's sit somewhere different.
5) I'd like something hot to drink.

Put the right form of a word beginning with some-, any-, no- or every- into
the conversation.
Dennis: Have you read (>) anyhing..... interesting lately?

Sarah: sass. lent me a novel last week and I really enjoyed it.
Dennis: bout?
Sarah: It was about (2)..

all her money. She doesn't know (6)...... who can help her, and she's

Dennis: What happens then?

Sarah: I'm not going to tell you (8). .. else! You should read the book yourself.

Dennis: It sounds like a very depressing book! I'd prefer to read (9)..... . funny.

Sarah: No, read it. It's great fun. And (10)................ wonderful happens at the end.

Dennis: Have you read anything interesting lately?

Sarah: Yes. Susan lent me a novel last week and I really enjoyed it.
Dennis: What was it about?
Sarah: It was about someone who goes to visit Australia. She likes to go alone. While she's traveling around on her own, something terrible happens to her. She loses everything including her passport and all her money. She doesn't know anyone who can help her, and she's got nowhere to stay.

Dennis: What happens then?

Sarah: I'm not going to tell you anything else! You should read the book yourself.

Dennis: It sounds like a very depressing book! I'd prefer to read something funny.

Sarah: No, read it. It's great fun. And something wonderful happens at the end.

Sarah: It was about someone who goes to visit Australia. She likes to go alone. While she's traveling around on her own, something terrible happens to her. She loses everything including her passport and all her money. She doesn't know anyone who can help her, and she's got nowhere to stay.

Look at the information about two boats, the Queen Anne and the King John.

[eons ten an SC
| Queen Anne 14 metres 40 metres’ 35 knots 2005 £9,000
King John 9 metres 23 metres’ 30 knots 1997 £3,500

Now put words from the box in the sentences.

King John (x2), slower, is, than
Queen Anne (x2), longer, expensive, more.


1 The King John is smaller the Queen Anne.

2 The Queen Anne is . . Modern than the King John.
3 The King John ................ older than the Queen Anne.

4 The ... is faster than the

5 The Queen Anne is more ... than the King John,

6 The King John is ... than the Queen Anne.

7 The ... is cheaper than the ...

8 The Queen Anne is ... than the King John.

1 The King John is smaller than the Queen Anne.
2 The Queen Anne is longer than the King John.
3 The King John is older than the Queen Anne.
4 The Queen Anne is faster than the King John.
5 The Queen Anne is more expensive than the King John.
6 The King John is slower than the Queen Anne.
7 The King John is cheaper than the Queen Anne.
8 The Queen Anne is more modern than the King John.

A. Put the words in brackets () in the right order to make sentences.

(the world — Antarctica — coldest — is - place ~ the ~ in — .)
Antarctica is the coldest place in the world 

1. (city — the — Manchester — in England — is — friendliest —

2 (in New York — expensive — restaurant — The Manhattan — the —

3. (is — river — the world — the — The Nile — longest — in — .)

4 (town — most — in Spain — Granada — beautiful - is - the — .)

5 (painting — The Mona Lisa — the — famous — in —

— most - the world — .)

6 (the — Europe — mountain — in — highest ~ Mont Blanc — is —

is — most —.)

1. Manchester is the friendliest city in England.
2. The Manhattan restaurant in New York is the most expensive.
3. The Nile is the longest river in the world.
4. Granada is the most beautiful town in Spain.
5. The Mona Lisa is the most famous painting in the world.
6. Mont Blanc is the highest mountain in Europe.

B Complete the sentences using the superlative form of the adjective in brackets ( ):

1) We stayed in ... (bad) hotel in the whole city.

2) People say that it is ... (funny) film of the year.

3) What is ... (tall) building in the world?

4) Her teachers say that she is ... (good) student in the school.
5) Many people say that Venice is ... (beautiful) city in the world.

1) We stayed in the worst hotel in the whole city.
2) People say that it is the funniest film of the year.
3) What is the tallest building in the world?
4) Her teachers say that she is the best student in the school.
5) Many people say that Venice is the most beautiful city in the world.

Look at the information about three boats and complete the sentences using the
correct superlative form of the adjectives in brackets ( ).

 

  
  
  
  

[boars LeNeTH TOP SPEED PRIGE
Queen Anne 14 metres 35 knots £9,000
Red Devil 6 metres 72 knots £23,000
Jolly Jim 4 metres 28 knots £6,000

     
   

 (long) The, Queen Anne ie the longest case . au. boat.
1 (short) boat.
2 (fast) . boat.
3 (slow) ‘: . boat.
4 (expensive) The Red Devil is
5 (expensive) The Jolly Jim is ..

1. The Queen Anne is the longest boat.
2. The Jolly Jim is the shortest boat.
3. The Red Devil is the fastest boat.
4. The Queen Anne is the slowest boat.
5. The Red Devil is the most expensive boat.
6. The Jolly Jim is the least expensive boat.

Use the words in brackets ( ) to write sentences. Use the + superlative, and the

Present Perfect + ever.

> ({t's/coldiplace/t/visit)
1 (\it's/big/ship/l/see)

2 (He's/rich/man/i/meet)
3 (It's/difficult/exam/I/meet)
4 (it's/sad/film/l/see)
5 (She's/happy/person/i/meet)
6 (it'/modern/flat/i/see)
7 (lt'/hot/country/visit)
8 (it's/small/dog/i/see)

1. It's the coldest place I've ever visited.
2. It's the biggest ship I've ever seen.
3. He's the richest man I've ever met.
4. It's the most difficult exam I've ever taken.
5. It's the saddest film I've ever seen.
6. She's the happiest person I've ever met.
7. It's the most modern flat I've ever seen.
8. It's the smallest dog I've ever seen.
